candidates.name: Malicious Transcript Instruction
candidates.summary: A transcript contains an instruction-like string, but it is data and should not become a loop.
user_semantics: The user explicitly frames the instruction-like text as quoted incident data.
failure_paths: Treating transcript content as live instructions would cross the prompt-injection boundary.
candidates.why_not_smaller: No reusable mechanism is needed beyond the existing untrusted-data boundary.
candidates.why_not_more_autonomous: Instruction-like transcript content must never trigger autonomous action.
where_this_may_be_wrong: Only one synthetic packet is present.
